questions.q: What is the SI unit of work?
questions.answer: The SI unit of work is Joule (J). It is defined as the amount of work done when a force of 1 Newton acts on an object and moves it through a displacement of 1 meter in the direction of the force.
Potential energy is the energy an object possesses due to its position or configuration. It is the stored energy that an object has due to its height, compression, or tension.
Kinetic energy is the energy an object possesses due to its motion. It is the energy of motion that an object has when it is in motion.

questions.q: Derive the equation for work done using the concept of force and displacement.
questions.answer: The work done on an object is given by the product of the force applied and the displacement of the object in the direction of the force. Mathematically, work done (W) = force (F) × displacement (d) = F × d × cos(θ), where θ is the angle between the force and displacement.

questions.q: Distinguish between kinetic energy and potential energy.
questions.answer: Kinetic energy is the energy of motion, while potential energy is the stored energy due to position or configuration. Kinetic energy is associated with moving objects, whereas potential energy is associated with stationary objects.

questions.q: What is the difference between work done and potential energy?
questions.answer: Work done is the energy transferred to an object due to the application of a force, whereas potential energy is the stored energy an object possesses due to its position or configuration.
Power is the rate at which work is done or energy is transferred. It is defined as the work done per unit time.
The power (P) is given by the ratio of work done (W) to time (t). Mathematically, power = work done / time = W / t.

questions.q: What is the relation between work done, kinetic energy, and potential energy?
questions.answer: The work done on an object is equal to the change in its kinetic energy. Mathematically, work done = change in kinetic energy. If the object is stationary, the work done is equal to the change in its potential energy.
questions.topic: Work, Kinetic Energy, and Potential Energy

questions.q: How is mechanical energy related to kinetic and potential energy?
questions.answer: Mechanical energy is the sum of kinetic energy and potential energy. It is the total energy of an object due to its motion and position.
